college.address: IIT Madras P.O., Sardar Patel Road, Chennai 600036, India

accreditation.validity_period: Current; governed by Board of Governors under IIT statutory framework

accreditation.notes: No institute-specific AICTE approval record was needed or verified for this statutory IIT; do not infer a conventional affiliated-college approval status.
Statutory IIT status is verified; a separate UGC category record was not located.

admission_routes.admission_process: Qualify JEE Main eligibility for JEE Advanced, obtain JEE Advanced rank, register and fill choices in JoSAA, accept allotted seat and complete institute reporting.
admission_routes.board_eligibility: Class XII with Physics, Chemistry and Mathematics; at least 75% aggregate (65% for SC/ST/PwD) or category-wise top 20 percentile, under official 2026 rules.
admission_routes.foreign_national_route: Direct JEE Advanced registration subject to official foreign-national rules; supernumerary allocation as applicable.
admission_routes.branch_change_rule: Discontinued from academic year 2024-25. Same-department dual-degree/IDDD and entrepreneurship pathways remain subject to CGPA/backlog rules.

location.official_address: IIT Madras P.O., Sardar Patel Road, Chennai 600036, India
location.nearest_bus_terminal: Adyar Gate / CLRI bus stops; Adyar bus depot nearby
location.technology_ecosystem: Adjacent to IIT Madras Research Park and near Taramani/OMR technology corridor; institute innovation and incubation ecosystem on/near campus.
location.cost_of_living_band: Moderate to high for off-campus Chennai housing; on-campus student accommodation is institution-priced.
location.data_origin: Official address/distances and transport descriptions from IITM; coordinates and local distances are map-derived approximations.

senior_junior_culture.summary: A recent public discussion describes seniors as approachable and ragging as absent, while official pages show structured freshie activities and anti-ragging mechanisms. Independent evidence is still too limited for a college-wide conclusion.
negative_themes: Only one recent detailed independent account

academic_culture.summary: Official sources describe relative grading, continuous assessment, high academic rigour and substantial elective flexibility. Student discussions suggest attendance flexibility but also say lectures remain important; the exact current attendance regulation needs official confirmation.
negative_themes: Attendance policy unclear from official public regulations
academic_culture.evidence_strength: Moderate institutional signal with limited student evidence

hostel_reality.summary: Official information confirms first-year sharing rooms, LAN in rooms, washing machines, common rooms, sports/music/tech spaces, and campus transport. Recent student comments agree that multiple mess categories exist but disagree sharply on food quality.
negative_themes: Mess quality repeatedly criticised in small public discussions
Room-sharing and hostel condition vary by building
hostel_reality.evidence_strength: Moderate repeated signal for mess variability; official verification for facilities

club_and_campus_life.summary: Strong institution-backed evidence shows a large, active ecosystem: CFI involved about 1,000 students in 2025, Shaastra and Saarang are major student-run festivals, and many Sangam clubs are officially listed. Activity dates for individual static-list clubs remain uneven.
negative_themes: Current recruitment and activity dates are not published for every listed club

coding_and_placement_culture.summary: Official placement reports show strong software, analytics, consulting, core-engineering and finance participation, while CFI provides a large project-building ecosystem. Company-wise role eligibility and non-CSE software access are not transparently published in the reviewed sources.
positive_themes: High CSE and EE CTC medians
negative_themes: Branch-wise placed denominators are often absent
New 2024–26 programmes have no graduating placement history
coding_and_placement_culture.evidence_strength: Strong official outcomes signal; limited student-reported process evidence

locality_and_student_life.summary: The main campus is a large protected-green residential environment in Chennai, linked by institute buses and city transport. Guindy metro and Kasturba Nagar MRTS require last-mile travel, while the Research Park and Taramani technology corridor create nearby industry exposure.
positive_themes: Near Research Park and Chennai technology corridor
negative_themes: Metro/rail stations are not at the hostel zone
locality_and_student_life.evidence_strength: Strong official location signal with limited anecdotal transit evidence

overall_satisfaction.summary: Available evidence points to unusually strong academic, innovation, festival and placement opportunities. The most repeated practical caution is variable mess experience; workload, competition and large-campus logistics may not suit every student. There is insufficient independent evidence to state how many students would choose IIT Madras again.
overall_satisfaction.evidence_strength: Moderate synthesis; no numeric rating justified

parent_mode_summary.summary: IIT Madras has exceptionally strong verified academic, ranking, placement and campus evidence. For a full-fee Indian B.Tech/B.S. student, the calculated four-year institute charge is about ₹9.40 lakh before hostel, mess and future revisions. First-semester new-admission hostel charges are ₹20,750 including a refundable ₹5,000 deposit, while Jul-Nov 2026 mess charges were still pending tender. Tuition remission and scholarships can materially reduce cost. Major parent checks are exact mess charge, programme fit for newly launched branches, and understanding that reported placement CTC is not guaranteed salary.

student_mode_summary.summary: The strongest student-facing advantages are flexible academics, research access, CFI, Shaastra/Saarang, wide recruiter participation and a large residential campus. Branch change is discontinued, so initial branch choice matters. New programmes such as CEM, iBME and BS Mathematics do not yet have graduating-batch placement outcomes. Hostel facilities are extensive, while recent public comments describe mess quality as variable.

decision_risks.severity: high for students choosing a branch only as a stepping stone

decision_risks.risk: New programmes lack graduating placement history
decision_risks.evidence: AI & Data Analytics, CEM, iBME and BS Mathematics are recent/current launches; historical placement reports predate graduates.

decision_risks.risk: Mess cost for Jul-Nov 2026 was not finalised in the current hostel circular

Relative grading and continuous assessment can create sustained workload
Scholarship awards may be limited and require current-year confirmation
decision_risks.severity: moderate for students relying on aid
decision_risks.evidence: Official scholarship catalogue includes limited-seat and renewal conditions.

decision_risks.risk: Large campus and last-mile transport from metro/rail stations
Mess quality evidence is conflicting and anecdotal

data_gaps: Advance dining/mess charge for Jul-Nov 2026 was not finalised in the published hostel circular.
Final 2025-26 placement outcomes are not yet published.
Company-wise roles, branch eligibility, selection counts and packages are not available for most recruiters.
Branch-specific placement denominators and placed counts are not published in the 2023-24 and 2024-25 CTC summary tables.
No verified institute-specific NAAC, NBA or conventional AICTE record was found; statutory IIT status is the authoritative institutional basis.
Current room-by-room hostel capacity, curfew details and ambulance availability were not verified.
Current recruitment/activity dates were not available for every static-listed club.
Independent student-experience evidence is too limited for numerical campus-culture ratings.
